unexpected_finds.subject: Three-program decomposition framing (A/B/C) provides new strategic lens
unexpected_finds.context: PROMPT 199 sec 'What you actually have' identifies three interlocking programs: A = PCF arithmetic stratification, B = BT/Newton-polygon backbone, C = AI Epistemic Governance Stack. Each addresses different community; each loses credibility the others provide when treated separately.
unexpected_finds.structural_insight: The umbrella v2.2 was implicitly treating these as one program with sub-axes. PROMPT 199 reframes as three programs in dialogue. This shifts strategy from 'extend umbrella to cover all' to 'each program needs its own flagship + cross-citation network'.
unexpected_finds.candidate_memory_fact: SIARC research output is three interlocking programs A (PCF arithmetic stratification) / B (BT-Newton-polygon analytic backbone) / C (AI Epistemic Governance Stack); each needs separate flagship + cross-citation network rather than unified umbrella treatment

unexpected_finds.subject: Flagship-paper-NOW assertion: closed results sufficient without Frontier-A completion
unexpected_finds.context: What-to-table bullet 3: 'Programs A and B have enough closed results to support a flagship paper RIGHT NOW; Route F closure would strengthen Move 2 but isn't required for Moves 1, 3, 4, 5, 6.'
unexpected_finds.structural_insight: This challenges the implicit prior that flagship publication waits on cascade completion. If true, the current cascade structure (Frontier-A->B->C standby) is over-engineered for the publication critical path. Flagship paper can fire in parallel with Frontier-A continuation; Frontier-A results fold into v2 or addendum.
unexpected_finds.verification_recommended: Re-read PCF-1 v1.3 + PCF-2 v1.4 + Logarithmic Ladder + Channel Theory + T2B + (4,2) + Finite-Depth Rigidity + BT baseline + D2-NOTE table-of-contents to confirm coverage of 7-slot flagship structure (Q-199 sec 'Move 1' items 1-7).

unexpected_finds.subject: Conference-talk-easier-than-journal asymmetry as institutional-affiliation friction workaround
unexpected_finds.context: Move 6 (a): 'Conference talk at major venue -- JMM 2027 / ICMS / OPSFA / Painlevé biennial -- *Much easier to get into than journals* and produce the social proof that makes journal acceptances easier.'
unexpected_finds.structural_insight: Current submission strategy is journal-first. Conference-first inverts this: talk acceptances -> social proof -> journal acceptances. This is a different scaling dynamic that may not have been considered in the 27-submissions / 14-deposits cadence.
unexpected_finds.operator_action: Survey upcoming relevant conferences (JMM 2027 deadlines, ICMS 2026/2027, OPSFA-16 deadlines, Painlevé biennial); add conference-submission track to admin axis post-RULE-1-lift.

unexpected_finds.subject: Direct outreach to 5-10 named researchers as Templeton-application complement
unexpected_finds.context: Move 5 (c) names specific researcher orbits: Adamczewski/Bugeaud (continued-fraction transcendence); Joshi/Mazzocco/Clarkson (Painlevé); Costin/Sauzin/Mitschi (resurgence); Bailey/Borwein-school (PSLQ-experimental). Suggests personalized notes with specific results they'd care about.
unexpected_finds.structural_insight: This is concrete, actionable, lower-overhead than awaiting Templeton verdict. Each named researcher has identifiable recent work the SIARC results could be positioned against. Outreach is unbinding (researcher can ignore) but creates citation-network seed crystals.
unexpected_finds.operator_action: Draft outreach matrix (researcher x relevant deposit x positioning angle); fire 1-2 outreach letters as pilot before full 10-researcher rollout.

unexpected_finds.subject: Provenance-churn-vs-content-milestone distinction as deposit-frequency discipline
unexpected_finds.context: What-to-table bullet 2: distinguishes umbrella revisions (provenance churn) from major content milestones (legitimate redeposit). Recommends bridge living-document + content-milestone snapshots only.
unexpected_finds.structural_insight: The 11 Zenodo deposits include several umbrella + provenance-tier records. Future deposit decisions should pass a 'content milestone vs provenance update?' test. Provenance updates -> bridge living-document; content milestones -> Zenodo redeposit.
unexpected_finds.candidate_memory_fact: Zenodo redeposits should be reserved for content milestones (new theorems, new sections, substantive revisions); provenance updates (version-tracking, cross-citation refreshes, axiom-reshape audits) belong in bridge living-document not Zenodo standalone records to avoid citation-graph dilution

unexpected_finds.subject: FREEFORM-CONSULT as legitimate dispatch class
unexpected_finds.context: PROMPT 199 arrived without CLI-drafted dispatch file. Operator initiated claude.ai consultation freeform; verdict pasted for absorption. D-199-3 captures the process-discipline question.
unexpected_finds.structural_insight: Two consultation modes exist: STRUCTURED-DISPATCH (CLI drafts PROMPT file -> Q-191-5 self-check -> operator paste -> verdict absorbed against structured Q-N) and FREEFORM-CONSULT (operator initiates claude.ai dialogue directly -> verdict absorbed against synth's natural sections). FREEFORM has lower overhead but loses Q-191-5 / bibliographic pre-verification / structured-question-numbering benefits. Both valid; choose by dispatch class.
unexpected_finds.candidate_memory_fact: SIARC supports two consultation modes: STRUCTURED-DISPATCH (CLI-drafted PROMPT with Q-191-5 self-check; for load-bearing math or process verdicts) and FREEFORM-CONSULT (operator-initiated claude.ai dialogue; lower overhead; for corpus-level strategic reframes or breadth-of-field consultations); absorb both at bridge with provenance tag
